title: Cochlidium connellii
taxonomy: Cochlidium connellii is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Cochlidium connellii places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales. The family Polypodiaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Within the family Polypodiaceae, Cochlidium connellii is part of the genus Cochlidium, characterized by its unique leaf morphology.
uses: Cochlidium connellii, a species of epiphytic fern, is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Caribbean region. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. In some cultures, Cochlidium connellii is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant. Further studies are required to fully explore its potential health benefits and applications.

intro: Cochlidium connellii, also known as Connelly's polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential in treating various health issues such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Cochlidium connellii include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cochlidium connellii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
